content.problemStatement:
A min-heap is a binary tree data structure that satisfies the heap property. Heap property refers to the specific ordering of elements in a binary heap. In a min heap, the value of any node is less than or equal to the values of its children.

The binary tree representing the heap is always complete. This means that the tree is completely filled at all levels except possibly the lowest, which is filled from left to right.

Min-heaps are often implemented using an array, where the children of the node at index i are located at indices 2i + 1 (left child) and 2i + 2 (right child). The parent of a node at index i is at index (i - 1) / 2.

Insertion in  Min Heap
In a binary heap, the elements are stored in an array
To insert a new element, you add it at the end of the array (as the last leaf in the binary tree).
Then, you compare the new element with its parent and swap them if necessary until the heap property is restored.
The pseudo-code given below shows how the numbers are added to your heap and how your heap is updated once a number is added.

`Class MinHeap:
    Public:
        heap: List of integers

        // Helper function to maintain the heap property by moving the element up
        Function heapifyUp(index):
            While index > 0:
                parentIndex = (index - 1) / 2
                If heap[index] < heap[parentIndex]:
                    Swap heap[index] with heap[parentIndex]
                    index = parentIndex
                Else:
                    Break

        // Function to insert a new element into the heap
        Function insert(value):
            Append value to heap
            Call heapifyUp(heap.size() - 1)

`Task
You are given an integer N (representing the count of elements) and N integers
You need to add these N integers in your heap
You have to print the array after each insertion
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains an integer N, the number of elements to insert into the min-heap. The next N lines each contain one integer to be inserted in the given order.
content.outputFormat: For each insertion, output a line containing the current heap array in level-order (the order stored in the underlying array). Numbers on the same line are separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each number is inserted into the min-heap and the heap property is restored by moving the new element up.
The numbers are already in non-decreasing order, so no swaps occur during heapify-up.
A single element forms a valid min-heap; the array contains just that element.

solution.approach: The problem requires implementing a min-heap and performing insertions while maintaining the heap property. The key insight is to use the heapify-up operation after each insertion to ensure the heap remains valid. The algorithm used is a simple iterative approach to heapify-up, swapping the newly inserted element with its parent if necessary, until the heap property is restored.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) total, where N is the number of elements to insert and T is the number of test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the heap array of size N.
